title: echium angustifolium subsp. expansum
intro: Echium angustifolium subsp. expansum, also known as the narrow-leaved or purple spike, is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, coughs, and skin problems. The health benefits of Echium angustifolium subsp. expansum include its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
